title: Vary grid gutters based on content relationships
description:
**Problem:** All grids use uniform gap-6 or gap-8. This makes everything feel equally spaced regardless of content relationship.

**Design Issue:** Spacing should communicate relationships - tighter between related items, looser between groups.

**Recommendation:**
- Tighter gaps within card content (gap-2, gap-4)
- Standard gaps in grids (gap-6)
- Larger gaps between sections (gap-12, gap-16)
- Consider varying column gaps vs row gaps

**Specific Examples:**
- Service card grid: gap-6 (good as is)
- Within cards: gap-2 for title/desc, gap-4 for list
- Pricing columns: gap-8 to separate options
- FAQ items: gap-8 for scannability

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Spacing varies based on content relationship
- [ ] Related content feels grouped
- [ ] Separate sections feel distinct
- [ ] Overall rhythm is intentional

title: Implement client authentication (magic links)
description:
Set up passwordless authentication for client portal.

## Architecture
```
Client requests login → Lambda generates magic link → SES sends email
Client clicks link → Lambda validates token → Sets session cookie
```

## Components
- Login page with email input
- Lambda function: generate and validate tokens
- Token storage (DynamoDB with TTL)
- Session management (JWT in httpOnly cookie)

## Flow
1. Client enters email on login page
2. Lambda generates unique token, stores in DynamoDB (15 min TTL)
3. SES sends email with magic link
4. Client clicks link → token validated → session created
5. Session cookie set for subsequent requests

## Security
- Tokens expire in 15 minutes
- Single-use tokens
- httpOnly, secure, sameSite cookies
- HTTPS only

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Login page accepts email
- [ ] Magic link email sent
- [ ] Link logs user in
- [ ] Session persists across requests
- [ ] Logout works

title: Create theme toggle component
description:
Create a toggle button component for switching between light and dark modes.

## Requirements
- Sun/moon icons (or similar) to indicate current mode
- Click to toggle between modes
- Accessible (keyboard navigation, aria labels)
- Smooth icon transition

## Behavior
1. On click: toggle `dark` class on `<html>` element
2. Save preference to localStorage (`theme: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'`)
3. On page load: 
   - Check localStorage for saved preference
   - If none, check `prefers-color-scheme` media query
   - Apply appropriate class before paint (prevent flash)

## Script Requirements
- Must run before page renders to prevent flash of wrong theme
- Should be inline in `<head>` or loaded with high priority
- Handle SSR (no window/document access at build time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toggle component renders in header
- [ ] Clicking toggles dark class on html
- [ ] Preference saved to localStorage
- [ ] System preference respected on first visit
- [ ] No flash of wrong theme on page load
- [ ] Keyboard accessible (Enter/Space to toggle)
- [ ] Screen reader announces state

title: Strengthen hero messaging with specific outcomes
description:
**Problem:** Current hero is generic - 'Practical technology consulting for small business' doesn't differentiate or create urgency.

title: Add specific CTAs instead of generic 'Contact Us'
description:
**Problem:** All CTAs are generic ('Get Started', 'Contact Us', 'Schedule a Consultation'). More specific CTAs convert better.

**Recommendation:** Customize CTAs by context:
- Services page: 'Get a Free Quote for [Service Name]'
- Pricing page: 'Book Your Free 30-Min Strategy Call'

title: Remove or reimagine wave SVG pattern in hero
description:
**Problem:** Wave SVG pattern at 10% opacity adds visual noise without contributing meaning or brand identity.

**Design Issue:** Decorative elements should either be distinctive enough to be memorable or removed entirely. The current wave is in an awkward middle ground.

## Options

### Option 1: Remove Entirely (Simplest)
Delete wave SVG, rely on gradient or solid background. Clean, minimal.

### Option 2: Enhance Current Wave
- Increase opacity to 20-30%
- Add subtle CSS animation (gentle float)
- Use as consistent element across pages

### Option 3: Replace with Distinctive Pattern

**Recommended Model:** Midjourney or manual SVG creation

**Geometric Grid Pattern Prompt:**
```
Subtle geometric grid pattern, interconnected nodes and lines, 
representing business connectivity, 
steel blue (#7b9dbb) on transparent background, 
10-15% opacity feel, minimal and professional, 
suitable for hero background, 
tileable/seamless pattern, vector style --ar 16:9 --tile --style raw
```

**Topographic/Flow Pattern Prompt:**
```
Abstract topographic contour lines, flowing organic curves, 
subtle data flow visualization aesthetic, 
single color steel blue (#7b9dbb), varying line weights, 
professional modern design, suitable for subtle background, 
seamless/tileable, clean vector style --ar 16:9 --tile --style raw
```

**Circuit/Tech Pattern Prompt:**
```
Minimal circuit board inspired pattern, clean geometric lines, 
subtle nodes and connections, modern tech aesthetic, 
steel blue on transparent, very subtle and professional, 
not busy or overwhelming, suitable for background texture, 
seamless pattern --ar 16:9 --tile --style raw
```

**Post-Processing:**
1. Trace to SVG (Vectorizer.ai or Illustrator)
2. Set stroke color to currentColor for theme support
3. Apply via CSS with opacity: 0.1-0.2
4. Consider subtle CSS animation (translateY or opacity pulse)

### Option 4: Gradient Only
Remove pattern, use more dramatic gradient:
```css
background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--light-bg) 0%, var(--soft-gray) 50%, var(--light-bg) 100%);
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Wave pattern either removed or purposeful
- [ ] Hero feels cleaner or more branded
- [ ] Decision documented for consistency
- [ ] Works in both light and dark mode
- [ ] If pattern kept: animates subtly or has clear brand purpose

title: Redesign cards with visual interest
description:
**Problem:** Cards use the most basic pattern: white box + border + shadow. This is indistinguishable from any template site.

**Design Issue:** Generic card styling doesn't contribute to brand identity or visual memorability.

**Recommendation Options:**
1. **Accent border:** Add 3px left border in steel-blue
2. **Gradient border:** Subtle gradient on border or background
3. **Icon accent:** Larger, more prominent icon treatment
4. **Asymmetric layout:** Vary card sizes in grid
5. **Hover transformation:** More dramatic hover state

**Suggested Implementation:**
- Add `border-l-4 border-steel-blue` to cards
- Or use gradient background that's barely visible
- Enhance hover: scale(1.02) + shadow-lg + translate

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Cards have distinctive visual treatment
- [ ] Design is consistent across all card types
- [ ] Hover states are engaging
- [ ] Cards contribute to brand identity
- [ ] Works in both light and dark mode

title: Add entrance animations for perceived quality
description:
**Problem:** The site is completely static, which feels dated compared to modern websites. No motion design creates a flat, template-like impression.

**Design Issue:** Static pages lack the polish that subtle animations provide. Users expect micro-interactions and entrance effects on professional sites.

**Recommendation:**
- Add scroll-triggered fade-in animations for sections
- Implement staggered entrance for card grids (services, pricing)
- Add subtle hover animations on buttons (translate + shadow)
- Consider parallax on hero section

**Technical Approach:**
- Use CSS `@keyframes` with Intersection Observer
- Or integrate a lightweight library like `aos` (Animate On Scroll)
- Keep animations subtle (200-400ms, ease-out)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Sections fade in as user scrolls down
- [ ] Card grids have staggered entrance animation
- [ ] Buttons have hover translate + shadow effect
- [ ] Animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion`
- [ ] No performance degradation (Lighthouse score maintained)
- [ ] Animations feel natural, not distracting

title: Visual asset generation guide for design refresh
description:
**Purpose:** Catalog of all visual assets needed for design refresh, with generation guidance.

## Brand Style Reference
- **Primary:** Steel Blue #7b9dbb
- **Secondary:** Navy #2d4966
- **Background Light:** #f5f5f5
- **Background Dark:** #1a2530
- **Font:** Inter
- **Aesthetic:** Professional, approachable, modern, minimal

## Asset Summary

| Story | Asset Type | Priority | Model Recommendation |
|-------|-----------|----------|---------------------|
| t8w | Service Icons | P1 | Use Heroicons/Lucide (no generation) |
| 1ft | OG Images | P2 | Figma/Canva OR Midjourney for backgrounds |
| zb2 | Hero Illustration | P2 | Midjourney v6 |
| ax7 | Background Pattern | P2 | Midjourney OR manual SVG |
| mgi | Hero Dark Variant | P2 | Photoshop recolor OR Midjourney |
| e3p | Section Icons | P3 | Use Heroicons/Lucide (no generation) |

## Recommended Workflow

### For Icons (t8w, e3p)
1. Browse heroicons.com or lucide.dev
2. Select icons matching concepts
3. Download SVG, customize color
4. No AI generation needed

### For OG Images (1ft)
1. Create Figma template (1200x630)
2. Generate abstract background with Midjourney
3. Composite in Figma with logo + text
4. Export optimized PNG

### For Hero Illustration (zb2, mgi)
1. Generate with Midjourney v6 using prompts in zb2
2. Generate both light and dark variants
3. Upscale and export
4. Optimize with Squoosh/TinyPNG

### For Background Pattern (ax7)
1. Decide: remove, enhance, or replace
2. If replacing: generate with Midjourney --tile flag
3. Trace to SVG with Vectorizer.ai
4. Implement as CSS background

## Midjourney Style Suffix
Add to all prompts for consistency:
```
--style raw --v 6 --no photorealistic, 3d render, photography
```

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] All assets use brand color palette
- [ ] Light and dark variants where needed
- [ ] Optimized file sizes (<300KB for large images)
- [ ] SVGs use currentColor where possible
- [ ] Tested on retina displays (2x exports)

## Asset Delivery Locations
- Icons: `src/components/icons/` (as Astro components)
- Hero images: `public/images/`
- OG images: `public/og/`
- Patterns: `public/images/patterns/` or inline SVG

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All required assets identified and documented
- [ ] Generation prompts tested and refined
- [ ] Assets generated and optimized
- [ ] Assets integrated into respective stories

title: Add depth and layering to dark mode UI
description:
**Problem:** Dark mode uses flat #1a2530 background with cards at the same visual level. Cards feel like they're floating in a void.

**Design Issue:** Modern dark UIs use subtle surface elevation - cards slightly lighter than background, creating depth hierarchy.

**Recommendation:**
- Implement layered surfaces in dark mode
- Background: #1a2530 (current)
- Cards/elevated surfaces: #243341 (slightly lighter)
- Hover states: #2d3d4d (lighter still)
- Add subtle border or shadow for definition

**Technical Approach:**
- Define dark mode surface colors: dark-bg, dark-surface, dark-surface-hover
- Apply to cards, modals, dropdowns
- Consider 1px border with low opacity white

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Dark mode has visible surface elevation
- [ ] Cards are distinguishable from background
- [ ] Hover states provide clear feedback
- [ ] Depth feels natural, not garish
- [ ] Consistent across all components

title: Add visual storytelling to About page bio
description:
**Problem:** About page bio is a wall of text. Long paragraphs are hard to scan and don't create visual interest.

**Design Issue:** Text-heavy pages need visual breaks to maintain engagement and aid comprehension.

## Content Structure
1. Brief intro paragraph
2. Pull quote: key philosophy statement
3. Background section with icon
4. Values section with icons
5. Call to action

## Asset Options

### Option A: Use Icon Library (Recommended)
Use Heroicons or Lucide for section icons:
- 👂 "We Listen First" → `ear` or `chat-bubble-left-right`
- 🎯 "Practical Solutions" → `target` or `check-badge`
- 🤝 "Partnership Approach" → `users` or `hand-raised`
- 💡 "Continuous Improvement" → `light-bulb` or `arrow-trending-up`

### Option B: Custom Spot Illustrations
Small illustrations (200x200) for each section.

**Recommended Model:** Midjourney v6

**Spot Illustration Style Prompt (apply to each):**
```
Simple spot illustration, [SUBJECT], 
minimal flat design, navy (#2d4966) and steel blue (#7b9dbb), 
clean vector aesthetic, professional but warm, 
white background, centered composition, 
small detail illustration suitable for web, 
no text --ar 1:1 --style raw --v 6
```

**Subjects:**
1. "person with ear tilted, listening intently, abstract representation"
2. "target with arrow, simple geometric, success metaphor"  
3. "two hands shaking, partnership symbol, professional"
4. "ascending graph with lightbulb, growth and ideas"

### Option C: Timeline Graphic
If including founder journey timeline:

**Timeline Prompt:**
```
Horizontal timeline infographic elements, 
milestone markers and connecting line, 
professional corporate style, 
navy and steel blue color scheme, 
clean minimal design, 
suitable for web, vector style --ar 3:1 --style raw
```

### Option D: Pull Quote Styling (No Assets)
Use typography and CSS only:
- Large quote marks in steel-blue
- Italic text, larger size
- Left border accent

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bio is broken into scannable sections
- [ ] At least one visual element (quote, icon, image)
- [ ] Key messages are highlighted
- [ ] Page is more engaging to scroll
- [ ] Content still flows narratively
- [ ] Visual elements match brand style

## Rationale
- Free
- No cookies required
- No consent banner needed
- Privacy-respecting
- Simple dashboard
- Aligns with brand values

## Trade-offs
- Less detailed than GA4
- No goal/conversion tracking
- Limited historical data

## Migration Path
Can add Plausible later if need more features.

## Status
**Accepted**

title: Refine typography with letter-spacing and line-height adjustments
description:
**Problem:** Typography uses Inter with default tracking, which looks loose at large sizes. Body text line-height is cramped for reading.

**Design Issue:** 
- Headlines lack the tightness that makes them feel crafted
- Subtitles don't differentiate from body text
- Long paragraphs (About bio) are hard to read

**Recommendation:**
1. **Headlines (h1, h2):** Add tracking-tight (-0.02em) for premium feel
2. **Body paragraphs:** Use leading-relaxed (1.625) for readability
3. **Subtitles:** Differentiate with muted color or all-caps + letter-spacing
4. **Consider:** Display font for hero headlines only (optional)

**Specific Changes:**
- h1: Add `tracking-tight` class
- h2: Add `tracking-tight` class
- Subtitle text: Add `text-navy/60` or `uppercase tracking-wide text-sm`
- About bio: Add `leading-relaxed` to paragraphs

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Headlines feel tighter and more intentional
- [ ] Body paragraphs are comfortable to read
- [ ] Subtitles are visually distinct from body text
- [ ] Typography hierarchy is clearer
- [ ] Changes applied consistently across all pages

title: Modernize form input styling
description:
**Problem:** Form inputs use basic borders with focus rings. This is functional but plain.

**Design Issue:** Contact forms are high-intent areas - they deserve polish that matches the rest of the brand.

**Recommendation:**
1. **Floating labels:** Labels animate from placeholder to above input
2. **Filled variant:** Light background (bg-gray-50) instead of white
3. **Animated focus:** Border color transition, subtle glow
4. **Custom select styling:** Replace browser default dropdown

**Specific Enhancements:**
- Focus state: Animate border-color (200ms transition)
- Add subtle background color to inputs
- Consider bottom-border-only style (more modern)
- Style textarea to match input fields

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Inputs have modern styling beyond default
- [ ] Focus states are smooth and visible
- [ ] Form feels cohesive with brand
- [ ] Accessibility maintained (visible focus)
- [ ] Works in both light and dark mode

title: Increase primary color saturation for stronger brand identity
description:
**Problem:** Steel blue (#7b9dbb) is too desaturated to be memorable. The palette reads "healthcare website" rather than "innovative tech consultant."

**Design Issue:** Low-saturation primary colors fail to create visual impact and brand recognition. CTAs don't command attention.

**Recommendation:**
- Increase saturation of primary accent color
- Consider shifting to #3b82f6 (Tailwind blue-500) or custom vibrant blue
- Alternatively, add a warm contrasting accent (coral/orange) for CTAs only
- Update steel-blue, medium-steel, and dark-steel values accordingly

**Color Options:**
1. Vibrant blue: #3b82f6 (more saturated, modern)
2. Keep blue + add accent: Steel blue for links, coral (#f97316) for CTAs
3. Shift palette: #4a90d9 (midpoint, more saturated but similar hue)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Primary color updated with higher saturation
- [ ] All CTAs use updated color and stand out visually
- [ ] Dark mode colors adjusted to match
- [ ] Contrast ratios meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for text)
- [ ] Color feels energetic but still professional

title: Add pricing calculator or ROI estimator
description:
**Problem:** Pricing page shows ranges but prospects can't estimate their specific cost.

**Recommendation:** Interactive element that:
- Asks a few questions about project scope
- Provides ballpark estimate
- Encourages contact for accurate quote

**Impact:** Lower priority but valuable for engagement

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Generates estimate with clear disclaimer

title: Vary section spacing and break the grid for visual interest
description:
**Problem:** Every section uses identical py-16/py-24 spacing, creating visual monotony. Nothing breaks the grid, making the site feel like a spreadsheet.

**Design Issue:** Uniform spacing and strict grid adherence produce a boring, template-like appearance. Modern design uses intentional asymmetry.

**Recommendation:**
- Vary section padding based on importance (hero: larger, dense info: tighter)
- Add overlapping elements that break container boundaries
- Use asymmetric layouts for some sections (image offset from grid)
- Consider full-bleed backgrounds with contained content

**Specific Changes:**
- Hero section: py-24 md:py-32 (more breathing room)
- CTA sections: py-12 md:py-16 (tighter, action-focused)
- Feature grids: py-16 md:py-20 (standard)
- Consider one section with -mt-12 overlap from previous

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] At least 3 different section padding values used
- [ ] At least one element breaks the grid (overlap or offset)
- [ ] Visual rhythm feels intentional, not random
- [ ] Spacing still works on mobile
- [ ] Overall page flow improved

title: Update hero image to support dark mode
description: **Problem:** Hero component always shows hero-light.png regardless of theme. Dark mode users see a jarring light image. ## Technical Implementation - Add dark mode variant similar to header logo handling - Use `dark:hidden` and `hidden dark:block` classes ## Asset Requirements ### If Current Hero is Photo-Based **Option A: Color adjustment of existing image** Use Photoshop/GIMP to create dark variant: 1. Duplicate hero-light.png 2. Apply curves adjustment (darken midtones/highlights) 3. Shift color temperature cooler 4. Adjust to complement dark background (#1a2530) 5. Save as hero-dark.png **Option B: AI recolor** Upload hero-light.png to Midjourney with: ``` [uploaded image] recolor for dark theme, darker tones, navy blue (#1a2530) compatible color palette, maintain same composition and elements, professional dark mode aesthetic --style raw ``` ### If Hero is Illustration (see zb2) Generate dark variant alongside light variant using prompts in issue zb2. ### If Hero Uses Gradient/Abstract Create CSS-only dark variant: ```css .hero-bg { background: linear-gradient(to-br, var(--light-bg), var(--soft-gray)); } .dark .hero-bg { background: linear-gradient(to-br, var(--dark-bg), var(--navy)); } ``` ## Implementation Code ```astro ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Hero image switches between light/dark variants based on theme - [ ] No flash of wrong image on page load - [ ] Dark variant complements dark theme colors - [ ] Both variants have similar visual weight

title: Reduce header visual weight to let hero dominate
description:
**Problem:** Header and hero use similar visual weights. The hero headline should dominate the viewport on load.

**Design Issue:** When header competes with hero, neither feels primary. First impressions are diluted.

**Recommendation:**
- Reduce header presence on scroll (smaller, transparent)
- Consider transparent header on hero sections
- Smaller logo on initial load, standard size on scroll
- Or: Move theme toggle to footer or less prominent position

**Technical Approach:**
- Add scroll listener to reduce header height
- Use transparent bg on hero, solid on scroll
- Reduce logo size: h-8 on initial, stays on scroll

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Hero headline is clearly the primary element on load
- [ ] Header doesn't compete for attention
- [ ] Header still accessible and functional
- [ ] Smooth transitions if animating
- [ ] Works on all pages, not just homepage

title: Redesign CTA buttons with contrasting accent color
description:
**Problem:** Steel blue CTAs on white don't have enough contrast to grab attention. Primary buttons look like secondary elements.

**Design Issue:** When the CTA color is the same as links and accents throughout, nothing stands out as "click me first."

**Recommendation:**
- Introduce a warm contrasting accent color for primary CTAs only
- Options: Coral (#f97316), Orange (#ea580c), or Amber (#f59e0b)
- Keep steel-blue for links and secondary buttons
- Add visual enhancements: subtle gradient, inner shadow, or hover animation

**Button Hierarchy:**
1. Primary CTA: Warm accent color (coral/orange)
2. Secondary: Steel-blue outline
3. Tertiary: Text link with underline

**Specific Enhancements:**
- Add translateY(-2px) + shadow increase on hover
- Consider subtle gradient (e.g., from-orange-500 to-orange-600)
- Ensure focus states are visible

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Primary CTAs use distinct accent color
- [ ] CTAs immediately draw the eye on each page
- [ ] Hover state provides clear feedback
- [ ] Contrast ratio meets WCAG AA
- [ ] Works in both light and dark mode
- [ ] Button hierarchy is visually clear

title: Fix text opacity for proper contrast and hierarchy
description:
**Problem:** Footer uses opacity-based text colors (text-soft-gray/70). This creates accessibility concerns and muddy appearance.

**Design Issue:** Opacity-based colors don't ensure contrast ratios. They also appear washed out and unprofessional.

**Recommendation:**
- Replace all opacity-based text colors with explicit color values
- Calculate proper color values that meet WCAG AA (4.5:1)
- Use explicit grays instead of white with opacity

**Specific Changes:**
- Replace `text-soft-gray/70` with calculated gray value
- Replace `text-white/80` with explicit light gray
- Verify all text meets 4.5:1 contrast minimum

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] No opacity-based text colors remain
- [ ] All text meets WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1)
- [ ] Footer text is clearly readable
- [ ] Visual hierarchy still works
- [ ] Consistent approach across light and dark modes

title: Replace emoji icons with consistent SVG icon set
description:
**Problem:** Service cards use emoji icons while navigation uses SVG stroke icons. Emojis render differently across platforms and look unprofessional.

**Design Issue:** Inconsistent iconography undermines visual cohesion. Emojis appear casual and render unpredictably on different devices/browsers.

## Asset Options

### Option A: Use Existing Icon Library (Recommended)
No generation needed. Use one of:
- **Heroicons** (heroicons.com) - Already similar to nav icons
- **Lucide** (lucide.dev) - Clean, consistent stroke icons
- **Phosphor** (phosphoricons.com) - Flexible weight system

Icon mappings:
- ⚙️ Custom Software → `cog-6-tooth` or `wrench-screwdriver`
- 🌐 Website Development → `globe-alt` or `computer-desktop`
- 🔗 System Integration → `link` or `arrows-right-left`
- 📊 SaaS Audit → `chart-bar` or `presentation-chart-line`
- 💡 Tech Consulting → `light-bulb` or `sparkles`

### Option B: Custom Generated Icons
If wanting unique branded icons, generate with:

**Recommended Model:** Midjourney or DALL-E 3

**Style Prompt for Each Icon:**
```
Minimal line icon, single stroke weight, [SUBJECT], simple geometric shapes, 
professional SaaS style, steel blue (#7b9dbb) on white background, 
consistent 2px stroke, 64x64 pixel grid, no fills, modern tech aesthetic, 
similar to Heroicons or Feather icons --ar 1:1 --style raw
```

**Subjects:**
1. "gear mechanism with subtle sparkle representing automation"
2. "browser window with code brackets"
3. "two puzzle pieces connecting"
4. "descending bar chart with magnifying glass"
5. "lightbulb with speech bubble"

**Post-Processing:**
- Trace to SVG using Vectorizer.ai or Adobe Illustrator
- Normalize stroke widths to 1.5-2px
- Ensure viewBox is 24x24 for consistency

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All service cards use SVG icons
- [ ] Icons use consistent style (stroke weight, size)
- [ ] Icons are accessible (aria-hidden="true" when decorative)
- [ ] Icons work in both light and dark mode
- [ ] Icons scale cleanly at all sizes
- [ ] Visual style matches navigation icons

title: Enhance footer with personality and engagement

title: Main Street Logic Website Launch
description:
Build mainstreetlogic.com - a modern website for a technology consultant serving small businesses.

## Business Context
Main Street Logic helps small businesses escape technology friction through:

title: Performance optimization and Core Web Vitals
description:
Optimize site performance for excellent user experience.

## Targets
- Largest Contentful Paint (LCP): <2.5s
- First Input Delay (FID): <100ms
- Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS): <0.1
- Lighthouse Performance: >90

## Optimization Checklist

### Images
- [ ] Convert to WebP/AVIF where supported
- [ ] Responsive images with srcset
- [ ] Lazy loading for below-fold
- [ ] Explicit width/height (prevent CLS)

### CSS/JS
- [ ] Astro ships zero JS by default ✓
- [ ] Purge unused CSS (Tailwind does this)
- [ ] Inline critical CSS if needed
- [ ] No render-blocking resources

### Fonts
- [ ] Subset fonts to needed characters
- [ ] font-display: swap
- [ ] Preload critical fonts
- [ ] Consider system fonts

### Caching
- [ ] CloudFront caching configured
- [ ] Cache-Control headers set
- [ ] Static assets have hashes

## Testing
- Lighthouse (Chrome DevTools)
- PageSpeed Insights
- WebPageTest

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All Core Web Vitals green
- [ ] Lighthouse Performance >90
- [ ] Page load <3s on 3G
- [ ] No layout shifts visible

title: Accessibility audit (WCAG 2.1 AA)
description:
Ensure site meets accessibility standards.

## WCAG 2.1 AA Checklist

### Perceivable
- [ ] Alt text on all images
- [ ] Color contrast ≥4.5:1 for text
- [ ] Information not conveyed by color alone
- [ ] Text resizable to 200%

### Operable
- [ ] All functionality via keyboard
- [ ] Visible focus indicators
- [ ] No keyboard traps
- [ ] Skip navigation link

### Understandable
- [ ] Language attribute on <html>
- [ ] Form labels associated with inputs
- [ ] Error messages clear and specific
- [ ] Consistent navigation

### Robust
- [ ] Valid HTML
- [ ] ARIA used correctly (if at all)
- [ ] Works with screen readers

## Testing Tools

title: ADR-003: Contact Form Backend
description:
Document contact form decision.

## Decision
**Lambda + SES** with honeypot spam protection.

## Context
Need spam-resistant contact form that's free/cheap and self-hosted.

## Options Considered
1. Lambda + SES (chosen)
2. Formspree
3. Netlify Forms
4. Google Forms
5. mailto link only

## Rationale
- No recurring cost (AWS free tier)
- Full control over data
- Honeypot is user-friendly (no CAPTCHA)
- Demonstrates serverless capability
- Can add features later (DynamoDB storage, etc.)

## Consequences
- More setup than SaaS options
- Need to handle SES verification
- Need to build/maintain Lambda code

## Status
**Accepted**

title: Improve hero section composition and visual impact
description:
**Problem:** Hero image sits politely in its grid column. The wave SVG pattern adds noise without purpose. Overall composition is static.

**Design Issue:** Hero is the most important real estate - it needs to command attention and set the brand apart.

## Layout Options (No New Assets)
1. **Overlapping layout:** Image extends into negative margin
2. **Background approach:** Full-bleed image with gradient overlay
3. **Animated elements:** Subtle floating or parallax on existing image

## Asset Option: Custom Illustration

If replacing photo with custom illustration:

**Recommended Model:** Midjourney v6

**Hero Illustration Prompt (Light Mode):**
```
Isometric illustration of small business ecosystem, main street storefront 
connected to floating digital tools and dashboards, warm friendly aesthetic, 
clean vector style, navy (#2d4966) and steel blue (#7b9dbb) with warm accents, 
professional but approachable, subtle connecting lines between elements, 
light cream/white background (#f5f5f5), modern flat design, 
no people faces, abstract representation of business technology, 
high quality, detailed but not cluttered --ar 16:9 --style raw --v 6
```

**Hero Illustration Prompt (Dark Mode):**
```
Isometric illustration of small business ecosystem, main street storefront 
connected to floating digital tools and dashboards, warm friendly aesthetic, 
clean vector style, steel blue (#7b9dbb) and soft gray (#d1cdc7) with subtle glows, 
professional but approachable, subtle connecting lines between elements, 
dark navy background (#1a2530), modern flat design, 
no people faces, abstract representation of business technology, 
high quality, detailed but not cluttered --ar 16:9 --style raw --v 6
```

**Alternative - Abstract Tech Pattern:**
```
Abstract flowing data visualization, curved lines connecting nodes, 
representing business integration and automation, 
steel blue (#7b9dbb) gradient to navy (#2d4966), 
clean modern aesthetic, subtle geometric shapes, 
technology consulting visual metaphor, 
professional corporate style, no text --ar 16:9 --style raw
```

**Post-Processing:**
- Export at 2x resolution (2560x1440 minimum)
- Optimize with TinyPNG/Squoosh
- Create light and dark variants
- Test contrast with overlaid text

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hero creates strong visual impact
- [ ] Composition feels intentional and branded
- [ ] Wave pattern either removed or purposeful
- [ ] Works well on all screen sizes
- [ ] Hero CTA stands out clearly
- [ ] Light and dark mode variants if needed

title: Build About page with story and approach
description:

## Content Sections

### Hero
'Empowering small businesses with intelligent automation is our mission at Main Street Logic.'

### Our History
Founded in 2025, Main Street Logic was created by seasoned technologists with deep experience in software engineering, artificial intelligence, and cloud-native architectures.

Before launching Main Street Logic, our team spent years modernizing Fortune 100 financial service organizations—bringing complex, distributed systems into the cloud, building microservice platforms, and developing AI-driven solutions at scale.

Now, we bring that expertise to Main Street—making modern technology accessible, effective, and personal for the businesses that power our communities.

### Locations We Serve
Main Street Logic is capable of providing solutions globally, but focuses on creating exceptional customer experiences in Richmond, Virginia and the surrounding area. We are available for in-person consultation if desired.

We run solutions for some clients directly in the cloud, taking advantage of superior scalability and reliability while building practical AI and automation solutions with cutting edge cloud provider solutions.

### What We Do
At Main Street Logic, we work directly with small business owners to uncover high-impact opportunities for automation. Our mission is to deliver practical, intelligent software solutions that simplify operations, reduce manual overhead, and unlock growth – without the complexity of enterprise systems.

We enable practical usage of AI solutions customized for your business such as automatic transcription, storage, and triage of voicemails, monitoring of social media reviews, website facelifts, Search Engine Optimization, and more.

## Design
- Clean, spacious layout matching brand mockups
- Section dividers with subtle wave backgrounds
- Responsive for mobile/desktop
